title: Steamed Eggplant Pad See Ew
description: A malty, crunchy Thai dish featuring steamed eggplants served with a flavorful Pad See Ew sauce. This solo dinner is a simple and delicious way to enjoy eggplants without the hassle of deep-frying.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced eggplants and cook until they start to soften, about 4 minutes.
Push the eggplants to one side of the wok and add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil.
Pour in the cooked rice noodles and stir-fry until they're well coated with the oil and slightly toasted.
Add the tamarind paste, soy sauce, and palm sugar, and stir-fry for another minute, until the sauce is well combined.
Add the cooked eggplants back into the wok and stir-fry everything together for about 2 minutes, until the eggplants are well coated with the sauce.
Push the eggplant mixture to one side of the wok and add a small amount of oil to the other side.
Crack in 2 eggs and scramble them until they're cooked through.
Mix the eggs with the eggplant mixture and stir-fry everything together for about 1 minute, until well combined.
Transfer the Pad See Ew to a serving dish and serve hot.
